hypothesis: Introducing a non-linear mixture generation protocol that simulates interaction-induced spectral changes (peak shifts, intensity changes, and cross-band coupling) into the training data will enable the architecture to disentangle and identify components in real-world mixtures with strong intermolecular interactions more accurately, yielding at least a 5 percentage-point improvement in Top-10 accuracy on ATR-IR mixture spectra relative to training solely on linearly combined spectra.
